title: ophrys insectifera subsp. subinsectifera
intro: Ophrys insectifera subsp. subinsectifera, also known as the Fly Orchid, is a subspecies of orchid that is native to the Mediterranean region and is characterized by its distinctive fly-like appearance. Historically, the Fly Orchid has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of the Fly Orchid are still being studied, but it is believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against infections and promote overall health. Herbal preparations of the Fly Orchid are typically made from the dried roots and leaves, and can be used as a tea or infused in oil for topical application, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
